data.title: Spyware Firm Ordered To Pay $167,250,000 in Damages for Enabling WhatsApp Hacks of 1,400 Smartphone Devices: Report
data.description:
An Israel-based cyber intelligence firm is reportedly ordered to pay damages for the security breach of the instant messaging platform WhatsApp six years ago. The NSO Group is behind the zero-click spyware Pegasus, which allows hackers to infiltrate smartphones without requiring user interaction, such as clicking a link or opening a message.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/05/08/spyware-firm-ordered-to-pay-167250000-in-damages-for-enabling-whatsapp-hacks-of-1400-smartphone-devices-report/

data.title: Are layer 2s good for Ethereum, or are they ‘extractive?’
data.description:
Layer 2s have been a great blockchain success story. They’ve reduced congestion on the Ethereum mainnet, driving down gas fees while preserving security. But maybe they’ve become too successful, drawing chain activity and fee income from the parent that spawned them? At least that’s what some are suggesting lately, most recently at Cornell Tech’s blockchain conference in late April. Indeed, some think Ethereum should be a little greedier, or at least fight harder for a bigger part of the revenue pie, particularly sequencing fees
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/layer-2s-good-for-ethereum-or-extractive?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Bitcoin hits $101k to reclaim six-figures as Trump confirms US, UK trade deal
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) reclaimed the $100,000 mark for the first time in over two months, driven by bullish market sentiment, rising institutional adoption, and renewed macroeconomic confidence after President Donald Trump announced a major trade deal with the UK on May 8.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/bitcoin-hits-101k-to-reclaim-six-figures-as-trump-confirms-us-uk-trade-deal/

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Price Reclaims $100K as Dominance Surges — New Bull Phase Underway?
data.description: Key Takeaways:Bitcoin breaks above $100,000 for the first time since January 2025, reaching $101,075.BTC market dominance crosses 60%, its highest since early 2021, signaling stronger investor confidence in Bitcoin over altcoins.Macroeconomic factors, renewed institutional inflows, and geopolitical news drive the rally.Bitcoin Hits $100,000 Milestone Again, Fueling Bullish MomentumBitcoin has reclaimed the $100,000 price level for the first time in nearly four months, reaching $101,075 on May 8 at 3:22 pm UTC, according to CoinGecko. The move represents a 4.2% increase from the day’s low of $95,967.This marks the third instance Bitcoin has crossed six figures:First on Dec. 5, 2024.Again on Jan. 20, 2025, ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.Most recently on May 8, 2025.Bitcoin Market Dominance Surpasses 60%Unlike the previous two breakouts, this latest surge coincides with Bitcoin’s market dominance rising above 60%, reflecting growing investor preference for BTC over altcoins. By comparison:BTC dominance was 52% in December 2024.Rose to 54% by January 2025.Surged to 60%+ in May, levels last seen in early 2021 when Bitcoin was climbing toward $60,000.This surge in dominance signals that altcoins may underperform in the near term, as capital flows consolidate around Bitcoin.Institutional Flows and Political Factors Drive the BreakoutThe rally follows a series of macroeconomic and political catalysts:Spot Bitcoin ETFs have seen $1.8 billion in inflows over the past trading week.Falling U.S. bond yields and a weakening dollar have added to Bitcoin’s appeal as a hedge.U.S. President Donald Trump hinted at a major trade deal, likely with the U.K., which analysts believe may be driving market optimism.“With gold also running good all year, there’s now a case for saying that Bitcoin may have proven itself as an economic hedge and a long-term store of value,” said Mercuryo CEO Petr Kozyakov.Analysts Eye $110,000 as Next TargetAccording to Ben Caselin, CMO at VALR, retail investors are still largely on the sidelines. This means the current rally is primarily institutional, which could drive a continued uptrend.“There is a good chance that Bitcoin will chart new highs north of $110,000 sooner rather than later,” Caselin stated, adding that Bitcoin could see a macro cycle top in Q4 2025.Key Events Ahead: CPI, Budget Data May Influence MomentumVincent Liu, CIO at Kronos Research, cautioned that upcoming U.S. macro data could influence BTC’s ability to sustain this breakout:U.S. Budget Data – May 12Consumer Price Index (CPI) – May 13“For the rally to sustain, the trade deal narrative will need to evolve into concrete progress,” Liu said.Bitcoin’s return to $100K signals growing investor conviction and renewed institutional interest. With dominance surging and ETF inflows accelerating, the foundation appears strong for a potential breakout beyond $110,000, barring macroeconomic surprises.

data.description:

The company cut its Bitcoin production by 75% and redirected resources to colocation and AI infrastructure services. In February, it signed a $1. 2B deal with CoreWeave to expand its data center. . .

data.title: U.S. Reduces Tariffs on UK Automotive and Metal Imports
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the United Kingdom government announced that the United States has significantly reduced tariffs on automotive imports from the UK. The tariffs, previously set at 27.5%, have been immediately lowered. Additionally, tariffs on steel and aluminum imports have been reduced to zero. This move is expected to enhance trade relations between the two countries and provide economic benefits to both parties.

data.title: U.S. and UK to Establish Aluminum and Steel Trade Zone
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States and the United Kingdom will create a trade zone for aluminum and steel. This initiative includes imposing a 10% tariff on British imports, aiming to generate $6 billion in external revenue. Additionally, the UK has agreed to a $10 billion deal to purchase Boeing aircraft.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Warns of Inflation Surge Amid Trade Tensions
data.description:
According to PANews, Goldman Sachs has issued a warning that U.S. President Donald Trump's global trade war could undermine significant progress made in combating inflation. The Wall Street bank informed clients in a report on Wednesday that a harmful combination of high tariffs and a weakening dollar is expected to cause key inflation indicators to surge in the coming months.

Goldman Sachs now anticipates that the annual rate of core inflation, excluding food and energy, will accelerate from 2.6% in March to 3.8% by December. This projection is based on the Federal Reserve's preferred PCE price index. The bank believes that the rise in prices will be much more pronounced than the Federal Reserve's March forecast, which did not account for the largest tariffs announced by the U.S. at the time. The Federal Reserve had projected a core PCE inflation rate of 2.8% for December.

Furthermore, Goldman Sachs expects the annual rate of core goods inflation to soar from 0.4% in March to 6.3% by December. By the end of the year, significant price increases are anticipated in sectors such as used cars (+8.3%), household appliances (+7.8%), video/audio/computers (+7.7%), jewelry/watches (+5.9%), and pharmaceuticals/medical (+7.8%).

data.title: Bitcoin's Evolving Role Amid Political and Technological Shifts
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin (BTC), often referred to as 'digital gold,' is experiencing a shift in its narrative as institutional interest grows and geopolitical landscapes change. The cryptocurrency, known for its scarcity, decentralization, and self-sovereignty, is now being considered for broader functionalities. In a recent discussion on The Clear Crypto Podcast, hosts Nathan Jeffay and Gareth Jenkinson spoke with entrepreneur Dan Held about Bitcoin's potential evolution. Held suggests that Bitcoin's next chapter could unlock new programmable use cases and messaging that extends beyond traditional crypto circles.

Political dynamics are also influencing Bitcoin's trajectory. U.S. President Donald Trump has openly supported Bitcoin and reportedly owns it, signaling a shift in regulatory and reputational perspectives. Held noted, "We have the most open administration toward Bitcoin in the United States." This marks a departure from previous narratives that often linked Bitcoin to environmental harm and illicit activities. Despite past challenges, such as China's mining crackdowns and the Biden administration's stringent crypto banking policies, approximately 25% of Americans now own Bitcoin, according to Held.

The future of Bitcoin may involve integrating decentralized finance (DeFi) tools, traditionally associated with blockchains like Ethereum or Solana. Held and Jenkinson advocate for building DeFi on Bitcoin, which could expand its use beyond mere speculation. This includes functionalities like borrowing, lending, and staking, allowing users to engage with Bitcoin in more complex ways. Jenkinson emphasized the potential of using Bitcoin as an underlying asset in DeFi, despite resistance from Bitcoin purists who prefer to maintain Bitcoin's traditional role. Held recalled the 2017 Bitcoin Cash fork as a contentious period but noted that current innovations are being developed without altering Bitcoin's fundamental rules. "No one’s proposing to change the rules of Bitcoin," he stated, highlighting that this evolution is built on existing structures.

For those interested in the full discussion, The Clear Crypto Podcast episode is available on Cointelegraph’s Podcasts page, Apple Podcasts, and Spotify. Cointelegraph also offers a variety of other shows for listeners to explore.
